content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
January 7,
1993
Darrell L.
Reese
International Association of Fire Fighters
Local 188,
IAFF
117 3rd Street
Rodeo, California
94572
Re:
Your Request Advice
Our File No.
A-92-495
Dear Mr.
Reese:
This is in response to your letter requesting assistance with
respect to the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”) .
You initially asked for general assistance,
thus,
we were treating your request as one for informal assistance.
Pursuant to your request on December 9,
1992,
we are providing
formal advice only to your second question which seeks
confirmation of telephone advice provided to you by Mr.
Elliot
Block.
QUESTION
Are funds raised by the Richiond Firefighters Union to
support the daily operating activities of the union reportable as
contributions?
CONCLUSION
Funds raised by the Richmond Firefighters Union to support
the daily operating activities of the union are not reportable
contributions provided the funds are not earmarked for the making
of contributions or expenditures.
1
Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95812 •
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
A-92-495
Page
2
FACTS
The Richmond Firefighters Union assesses
$10 per month on
members to develop additional funds for the union.
The monies are
placed in a general account,
and part of the funds are transferred
to two campaign committees from which the union supports or
opposes candidates and measures.
The union is contemplating
raising funds from businesses and others to support the daily
activities of the Richmond Firefighters.
The new funds would be
deposited into a separate newly established account and all of the
funds would be used to pay for the every day operating expenses of
the union.
None of the funds would be used nor earmarked for
political activities.
The new money,
however,
would impact the
union’s political activities by freeing up the membership dues and
assessment monies for its political activities.
iS CUS SI ON
Section 82015 provides in pertinent part:
“Contribution” means a payment,
a forgiveness
of a loan,
a payment of a loan by a third party,
or
an enforceable promise to make a payment except to
the extent that full and adequate consideration is
received unless it is clear from the surrounding
circumstances that it is not made for political
purposes.
Furthermore, Regulation 18215(c)
provides:
(C)
“Contribution” includes any monetary or
norimonetary payment for which full and adequate
consideration is not made to the donor that is
received by any person or organization other than a
candidate,
controlled committee, official committee
of a political party,
or organization formed or
existing primarily for pplitical purposes if the
payment is “earmarked” for the making of
contributions or expenditures.
A payment is
“earmarked” when, at the time of making the
payment, the donor knows or has reason to know that
the payment or funds with which the payment will be
commingled will be used to make contributions,
as
defined in Government Code Section 82015 and this
regulation,
or expenditures,
as defined in
Government Code Section 82025 and
2 Cal.
Adm.
Code
Section 18225.
If the donor knows or has reason to
know that only part of the payment will be used to
make contributions or expenditures,
the payment


Our File No.
A-92-495
Page
3
shall be apportioned on a reasonable basis in order
to determine the amount of the contribution.
Fac
tors relevant to a determination that the donor has
reason to know that all or part of the payment will
be used to make expenditures and contributions
include but are not limited to the established
practice of the person or organization with respect
to expenditures and contributions and any
representations made when the payment is solicited.
(Emphasis added.)
As indicated in Regulation 18215,
a payment is eIearmarkedI
when, at the time of making a payment,
the donor knows or has
reason to know that a portion of his or her dues will be used to
make contributions or expenditures for political purposes.
Factors relevant to a determination that the donor has reason to
know that part of the dues will be used for political purposes
include the established practice of the organization and
representations made when the payment is solicited.
(Laddish
Advice Letter,
No.
A—76-512.)
Therefore, if no representations
are made that the funds will be earmarked for political purposes
and they are not used for the making of contributions or
expenditures,
funds raised by the Richmond Firefighters Union to
support the daily operating activities of the union are not
reportable contributions.
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322_5901.2
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Assistant General Counsel
By:
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel,
Legal Division
SH:LN
2
Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively,
copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of lO per page.
